hero.title: Start Your Projects.
hero.subtitle: Practical strategies for ADHD developers to overcome project paralysis and build momentum.

dopamine.title: Dopamine Dysregulation
dopamine.description: Your brain prioritizes immediate rewards over long-term goals. New projects feel exciting (dopamine spike), but routine work feels impossible.

executive.title: Executive Function Deficit
executive.description: Starting tasks requires more mental energy for ADHD brains. Once the initial stimulus wears off, momentum collapses.

working.title: Working Memory Overload
working.description: You lose track of what you've built, why you built it, and what comes next. Context-switching costs are extremely high.

perfectionism.title: Perfectionism + Shame Spiral
perfectionism.description: One mistake or setback triggers avoidance. You'd rather not start than risk failure. The guilt makes it worse.

strategies.title: 4 Core Strategies
strategies.subtitle: Proven tactics to overcome ADHD project paralysis

psychological.title: Psychological Strategy
psychological.description: Reframe failure as system debugging, not personal failure. Separate your worth from your productivity.
items: Treat mistakes as data, not shame

behavioral.title: Behavioral Strategy
behavioral.description: Design your work to match how your brain actually works, not how it should work.
items: Externalize everything (write it down, don't rely on memory)
Use Pomodoro timers (25 min focus + 5 min break)
Pair tasks with immediate rewards (dopamine pairing)

environment.title: Environment Design
environment.description: Create sensory anchors that trigger focus mode instantly.
items: Work outside your home (coffee shop, library, coworking)
Use consistent music/soundscapes (same playlist = focus trigger)

execution.title: Execution System
execution.description: A structured daily routine that works WITH your ADHD brain, not against it.
items: Daily Starter Quest (10 min minimum commitment)

items.q: Will this work if I haven't been diagnosed with ADHD?
items.a: Yes. These strategies work for anyone with executive function challenges, procrastination, or project paralysis. ADHD is just one cause.

items.q: What if I'm already on ADHD medication?
items.a: Great. Medication helps with dopamine regulation, but it doesn't replace structure. Combine medication with these strategies for best results.

items.q: How long until I see results?
items.a: You should feel momentum shift within 2-3 weeks if you follow the system consistently. Real progress (completed projects) takes 6-8 weeks.

items.q: What if I fail the first week?
items.a: That's not failure—that's data. Adjust your system. Maybe your Starter Quest is too big. Maybe your focus zone isn't working. Iterate.

items.q: Can I use this for non-coding projects?
items.a: Absolutely. These strategies work for writing, design, music, any creative or technical work that requires sustained focus.

cta.title: You Can Finish This
cta.subtitle: ADHD isn't a character flaw. It's a different way your brain works. With the right system, you can build momentum and complete your projects.

footer.description: This guide is based on research-backed strategies for ADHD and executive function.

executionPlan.title: Your 6-Week Roadmap
executionPlan.subtitle: A structured plan to implement ADHD strategies and complete your project

phase1.title: Foundation (Week 1)
phase1.description: Set up your system and understand your project

audit.label: Code Audit (15 min)
audit.description: Review your project structure and identify what you've built

architecture.label: Document Architecture (30 min)
architecture.description: Create ARCHITECTURE.md with tech stack and component hierarchy

kanban.label: Set Up Kanban Board
kanban.description: Create visual task management (Trello, Notion, GitHub Projects)

phase2.title: Execution (Weeks 2-6)
phase2.description: Build momentum and complete your project

focusZone.label: Design Your Focus Zone
focusZone.description: Prepare environment with sensory cues (music, scent, etc.)

starterQuest.label: Daily Starter Quest (25 min)
starterQuest.description: Pick ONE small task from "Next Up" column and complete it

weeklyRetro.label: Weekly Retro & Adjust
weeklyRetro.description: Review what worked and what didn't every Friday

dailyRoutine.title: Your Daily Routine
dailyRoutine.subtitle: Follow this structure every day

steps.title: Morning Setup (5 min)
steps.description: Activate your focus zone: music, scent, location, etc.

steps.title: Starter Quest (25 min)
steps.description: Pick ONE micro-task from "Next Up" and complete it using Pomodoro timer

steps.title: Celebrate & Break (5 min)
steps.description: Move task to "Done". Enjoy your reward. Take a real break.

steps.title: Repeat or Extend (Optional)
steps.description: If momentum is high, do another Pomodoro. Otherwise, that's your win for today.

dailyRoutine.note: This routine takes 35 minutes minimum. That's your daily commitment. Anything more is a bonus.

weeklyRetro.title: Weekly Retro Template
weeklyRetro.subtitle: Every Friday, ask yourself these questions

items.title: What worked this week?
items.description: Which strategies helped you stay on track?

items.title: What didn't work?
items.description: Where did you get stuck or lose momentum?

items.title: What will you adjust?
items.description: What change will you make next week?

items.title: Progress snapshot
items.description: How many tasks did you complete? How do you feel?

energyLog.title: Energy Log (Track for 2 Weeks)
energyLog.subtitle: Identify your peak productivity times
energyLog.note: Once you identify your peak times, schedule complex work (debugging, new features) during those hours.

cta.title: Ready to Start?
cta.subtitle: Print this page or bookmark it. Start with Phase 1 this week. You've got this.
